hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — 7, 3. Your opponent raised before the flop, putting the pressure on, and you decided to call.
Flop — 7, 9, King. Your opponent bet, and you chose to call to see the next card.
Turn — 4. The board didn't change much for you, and your opponent bet again, this time with a bigger wager, putting you in a tough spot.
River — King. The final card sparked a large river bet from your opponent, leaving you to make a crucial decision.

wrongReasons: 7 2 is close, but the river lead is the tell. The correct hand picked up more on the river than 7 2 would have.
K 6 makes a three of a kind here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
Q 3 has little reason to lead the river here — one pair can't comfortably go for value after calling two streets.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 6 7

Your opponent started with a raise and maintained pressure on each street, but the key to their hand is the improvement on the river to Two Pair with the paired King and their hole card, the 7. This consistent betting pattern, especially the larger turn bet, indicates a hand that's strong but not invincible, fitting the 6 7 hole cards perfectly.
